rules.title: Systemic fixes only
rules.summary: Always prefer systemic fixes over ad hoc, bandaid, short-lived, or partial fixes.
rules.rationale: Architext's recurring failures have come from fixing one renderer, command, or view while leaving the same policy duplicated elsewhere.

rules.title: Architecture and docs before code
rules.summary: Begin substantial changes with architecture analysis and documentation before implementation.
rules.rationale: Architext is itself architecture tooling; unexplained implementation drift undermines the project.

rules.title: Target repositories stay data-only
rules.summary: Target repositories commit architecture data and lifecycle metadata, not copied Architext viewer, schema, tool, package, or Vite files.
rules.rationale: Global CLI distribution only works if upgrades happen in the installed package rather than in vendored target copies.

rules.title: Release Truth stays current
rules.summary: When release scope, implementation state, blockers, or deferrals change, update Release Truth in the same work session.
rules.rationale: Release Truth exists so users do not have to infer agent release decisions from chat history.

rules.title: Single paths to success
rules.summary: Do not create parallel mechanisms for the same behavior; converge duplicated lifecycle, routing, rendering, and validation logic.
rules.rationale: Duplicated behavior has caused inconsistent routing, step selection, doctor/sync behavior, and release planning state.

rules.title: Schema change discipline
rules.summary: Purely additive schema changes may ship in a minor release; breaking schema changes require a major semver release and migration path.
rules.rationale: Data-only repositories depend on Architext to preserve and migrate user-owned JSON safely.

rules.title: Agents maintain JSON truth
rules.summary: Agents should update reviewed Architext JSON data and validate it, not leave project truth only in prose.
rules.rationale: The viewer and CLI can only help if the source data is current and valid.
